purpose: Compare the latest AP16 candidate-fact scaling smoke with the accepted pair-sign producer hierarchy and choose the memory-safe production route.
decision: Do not scale the AP16 positive-survivor candidate-fact route as production evidence. Keep it as bounded smoke/regression infrastructure only. Resume implementation on the pair-sign producer membership bridge, whose theorem surface is semantic and whose AP16AO projection is far below the current memory and build-time gates.
notes: The candidate-fact route still proves useful bounded facts, but its heavy shared candidate module grows with concrete positive-mask proof blocks and reached 8.015 GiB RSS for only 60 positive-mask facts.
The pair-sign producer hierarchy keeps source and row producers private, exports semantic coverage, and avoids concrete proof replay over positive masks.
Further focused builds should use scripts/run_memory_guarded.py and avoid ulimit -v, which caused false Lean thread-creation failures in AP16AN.
